open_positions.name: Extreme Networks
open_positions.thesis: Sympathy selling off CALX print - both are networking/access equipment names. EXTR down 5.8% on 3x volume with no company-specific news in feed, while XLK is up 2.2%, strongly suggests read-through trade. Catalyst: CALX -14% print dragging networking peers. Falsified by: Discovery of EXTR-specific negative catalyst (pre-announce, lost deal, downgrade) would validate the move; continued sector-wide weakness would suggest broader enterprise IT spend concern.
open_positions.latest_reasoning: Entry was premised on sympathy selling following CALX -14% print, with falsification conditions being (1) EXTR-specific negative news or (2) broad sector weakness. Neither has materialized: XLK remains up 2.2%, no downgrade or pre-announce has hit the wire for EXTR, and the stock is up 17.96% from entry. The sympathy move appears to have been correctly identified and already priced in. Horizon was 'days' and we're only 7 days out; no catalyst exhaustion yet. Position is profitable, position size is well within limits (17.6% of equity at current prices), and the original thesis—that the CALX selloff was overdone for EXTR as a collateral victim—has held. No reason to sell into strength.

trade_log.why: Opened per flag: LIKELY OVERDONE conf 3 — CALX -14% print dragging networking peers
